title: სახელმწიფო უსაფრთხოების სამსახურის შესახებ საქართველოს კანონი — კიბერუსაფრთხოების ბიურო
title_en: Law of Georgia on State Security Service — Cyber Security Bureau Provisions
description: The Cyber Security Bureau (CSB) of the State Security Service of Georgia is established by the Law on State Security Service and operates as Georgia's national cybersecurity authority for critical and classified systems. The CSB coordinates national responses to major cybersecurity incidents, oversees the security of state information resources, and serves as the interface with foreign intelligence services and international cybersecurity organizations on sensitive matters. The CSB works alongside CERT-Georgia, which handles civilian cybersecurity incidents.

provisions.title: Article 1. Status and Mandate of the Cyber Security Bureau
provisions.content: The Cyber Security Bureau (CSB) is a structural subdivision of the State Security Service of Georgia established to protect Georgia's critical information infrastructure, classified state information systems, and national information security interests against cyber threats. The CSB exercises the following principal functions: (a) developing and implementing national cybersecurity policy in respect of critical and classified information systems; (b) coordinating the national response to significant cybersecurity incidents affecting national security; (c) conducting technical investigations of cyber attacks against critical national information infrastructure; (d) providing cyber threat intelligence to relevant state bodies; (e) coordinating with foreign cybersecurity agencies and intelligence services on cyber threats; and (f) advising government bodies on cybersecurity matters. The CSB shall work in coordination with CERT-Georgia, which handles cybersecurity incidents in the civilian sector, and with sectoral regulatory authorities. Personnel of the CSB are subject to the Law of Georgia on State Security Service and relevant secrecy obligations.

provisions.title: Article 2. Authority Over Classified Systems
provisions.content: The CSB is the competent authority for the security of information systems used by state bodies to process classified information. The CSB shall: (a) establish security standards and technical requirements for classified information systems; (b) certify and accredit classified information systems before they are put into operation; (c) conduct inspections and audits of classified information systems; (d) advise state bodies on the protection of classified information processed in electronic form; and (e) investigate security incidents involving classified information systems. State bodies shall obtain CSB approval before introducing new classified information systems or significantly modifying existing ones. The CSB shall maintain a register of accredited classified information systems and shall report annually to the head of the State Security Service on the security status of classified systems.

provisions.title: Article 3. National Cyber Incident Response
provisions.content: The CSB shall coordinate Georgia's national response to cybersecurity incidents of national significance. Where an incident or series of incidents affecting critical infrastructure or national security occurs or is imminent, the CSB may: (a) activate the National Cyber Incident Response Plan; (b) direct all relevant state bodies to implement specified cybersecurity measures; (c) mobilize technical resources and personnel from state bodies to assist affected operators; (d) request assistance from foreign partners and international organizations; and (e) advise the Government and the President on the nature and consequences of the incident and on necessary response measures. The CSB shall maintain a 24/7 capability to receive reports of and respond to significant cybersecurity incidents. The CSB shall conduct post-incident reviews of significant incidents and shall publish unclassified summaries of lessons learned.

provisions.title: Article 4. Cyber Threat Intelligence
provisions.content: The CSB shall collect, analyze, and disseminate intelligence on cyber threats to critical national information infrastructure and state interests. Cyber threat intelligence activities shall be conducted in accordance with Georgian law, including the Law on Operative-Investigative Activity and the Law on Intelligence Service. The CSB shall share relevant threat intelligence with: (a) other state security bodies; (b) CERT-Georgia; (c) operators of critical information systems, where appropriate; and (d) allied intelligence and security services, under applicable international agreements. Intelligence sharing with the private sector shall be subject to appropriate confidentiality protections and shall be used only for defensive purposes. The CSB shall participate in international intelligence sharing arrangements relating to cyber threats, in accordance with Georgia's foreign policy and international commitments.

provisions.title: Article 5. Cooperation with CERT-Georgia
provisions.content: The CSB and CERT-Georgia shall coordinate their activities through a formal cooperation framework, including: (a) regular meetings and information sharing at the operational level; (b) joint incident response protocols defining the division of responsibilities for incidents affecting both national security and civilian systems; (c) joint exercises and training programs; and (d) shared access to relevant threat intelligence, subject to applicable classification restrictions. The CSB shall refer incidents that do not have national security implications to CERT-Georgia for handling, and CERT-Georgia shall refer incidents with potential national security implications to the CSB. The cooperation framework shall be reviewed annually and shall be adapted as necessary to reflect changes in the threat environment and in the roles of the respective bodies.

provisions.title: Article 6. International Cooperation
provisions.content: The CSB shall cooperate with foreign cybersecurity agencies, intelligence services, and international organizations on matters relating to cybersecurity, subject to Georgian law and applicable international agreements. The CSB shall maintain liaison relationships with: (a) the cybersecurity agencies of EU member states and EU institutions, including ENISA; (b) the Cybersecurity Coordination Centre and National Coordination Centres established under the EU Cybersecurity Regulation; (c) NATO cybersecurity bodies, in accordance with Georgia's aspirations for NATO membership; (d) the Council of Europe bodies relevant to cybersecurity; and (e) the cybersecurity agencies of partner countries with which Georgia has bilateral cooperation agreements. The CSB shall participate in international cybersecurity exercises and shall contribute to the development of international cybersecurity norms and standards. International cooperation shall be conducted in a manner consistent with Georgia's foreign policy objectives and its EU approximation commitments.

provisions.title: Article 7. Cybersecurity Research and Development
provisions.content: The CSB shall promote cybersecurity research and development in Georgia, in cooperation with academic institutions, research centers, and the private sector. The CSB shall: (a) identify and prioritize cybersecurity research needs of relevance to Georgia's national security; (b) support the development of indigenous cybersecurity capabilities; (c) cooperate with Georgian universities and research institutes on cybersecurity research projects; (d) promote the development of a cybersecurity industry and ecosystem in Georgia; and (e) develop and deliver cybersecurity training for state security personnel. The CSB shall cooperate with EU research bodies, including ENISA and Horizon Europe programs, on cybersecurity research and innovation. Georgia's national cybersecurity research agenda shall be aligned with EU cybersecurity research priorities, as part of Georgia's EU integration process.

provisions.title: Article 8. Parliamentary Oversight
provisions.content: The activities of the CSB relating to cybersecurity shall be subject to parliamentary oversight through the relevant committee of the Parliament of Georgia. The CSB shall submit to the Parliament an annual classified report on: (a) significant cybersecurity incidents affecting national security; (b) the state of cybersecurity of critical and classified information systems; (c) cyber threats facing Georgia; and (d) the CSB's activities and capabilities. An unclassified version of the annual report shall be made available to the public. The Parliament may request additional information from the CSB on cybersecurity matters through the relevant committee. The CSB's budget shall be subject to parliamentary approval and independent audit.

provisions.title: Article 9. Cybersecurity Workforce
provisions.content: The CSB shall develop and maintain a skilled cybersecurity workforce capable of defending Georgian critical infrastructure and classified systems against sophisticated cyber threats. The CSB shall: (a) establish qualification requirements for cybersecurity personnel in the state security services; (b) develop and deliver specialized cybersecurity training programs; (c) coordinate with the Ministry of Education and Science on cybersecurity education programs at universities; (d) promote the recruitment of cybersecurity talent from academic institutions and the private sector; and (e) participate in international training programs and exchanges with allied cybersecurity agencies. The CSB shall develop a national cybersecurity workforce development strategy in coordination with relevant ministries and with the private sector. Cybersecurity personnel of the CSB shall be subject to regular security vetting and to continuing professional development requirements.

provisions.title: Article 10. National Cybersecurity Exercises
provisions.content: The CSB, in coordination with CERT-Georgia and sector regulators, shall organize and conduct national cybersecurity exercises on a regular basis. National exercises shall: (a) test the effectiveness of national cybersecurity incident response capabilities; (b) assess the preparedness of operators of critical information systems; (c) identify gaps in national cybersecurity capabilities and procedures; and (d) build resilience and readiness across critical sectors. The CSB shall participate in international cybersecurity exercises organized by the EU, NATO, and other international bodies. Operators of critical information systems designated under the Law on Critical Information System Security shall be required to participate in national exercises organized by the CSB or by sector regulators. The CSB shall publish an unclassified summary of the lessons learned from national exercises.

definitions.definition: A structural subdivision of the State Security Service of Georgia established to protect Georgia's critical information infrastructure, classified state information systems, and national information security interests against cyber threats.
An information system used by state bodies to process information classified as a state secret or as restricted information under Georgian law.
